reasoning: Tool choice is appropriate — get_traditional_arts is the canonical tool for 職人技/伝統工芸 queries. The cross_program_advisory surfaces the correct subcategory with intent_keywords including '職人技' and '職人', and canonical entities (Wajima lacquerware, Nishijin silk, Bizen pottery, Kutani porcelain) are named with authoritative METI/UNESCO ICH designations. Intent score is 4 (not 5) because the 'forgotten / dying' nuance — the core emotional framing of the query — is not addressed; no endangered-craft filter or dying-tradition tagging is surfaced. No RULE B double-counting applied; no cluster fired so RULE E does not apply. All dims ≥4 so failure_category is null.
missing_data: Endangered or dying-craft metadata (e.g. records flagged as having fewer than N active practitioners, or official 'at risk' designations from 文化庁) would directly address the 'forgotten' framing of the query. A keyword=職人 or keyword=伝統工芸 filtered call returning live practitioner counts or workshop access info would strengthen practical_usefulness.
improvement_hint: Add a dying_craft or practitioner_count field to METI 伝統的工芸品 records where official data exists (some METI records include successor-shortage warnings). Surfacing this in the cross_program_advisory or as a filterable field would let the agent highlight genuinely endangered crafts rather than all designated crafts equally.
